name: 34_Customer_Segmentation_KMeans_CustomerSegmentation_ML
query: I need to create a customer segmentation system using the K-means clustering algorithm with the Kaggle Customer Segmentation dataset. Start by standardizing the data in `src/data_loader.py`, then use the elbow method to determine the optimal number of clusters and save the elbow plot to `results/figures/elbow.jpg`. Implement the K-means algorithm in `src/model.py`. Save the cluster centers in `results/metrics/cluster_centers.txt`. Visualize the segmentation results using seaborn and save the plot as `results/figures/customer_segmentation.png`. Create an interactive Dash dashboard allowing dynamic exploration of the segments.

requirements.criteria: The "Kaggle Customer Segmentation" dataset is used, including data loading and preparation in `src/data_loader.py`.
Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.
The elbow method is used to determine the optimal number of clusters. Please save the elbow plot to `results/figures/elbow.jpg`.
The K-means clustering algorithm is implemented in `src/model.py`.
The Customer segmentation is visualized using "seaborn," with the plot saved as `results/figures/customer_segmentation.png`.
An interactive dashboard which allows dynamic exploration of the segments is created using "Dash".

preferences.criteria: The elbow plot clearly shows how the optimal number of clusters is determined.
The system properly manages the launch and termination of the dashboard.

judge_stats.criteria: The "Kaggle Customer Segmentation" dataset is used, including data loading and preparation in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not actually use or load the "Kaggle Customer Segmentation" dataset. The file only contains placeholder logic: `load_dataset()` returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and there is no dataset path, file reading (e.g., pandas `read_csv`), schema handling, or preparation code. Although the string `"Kaggle Customer Segmentation"` appears in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS`, that is only descriptive text, not evidence of real dataset usage or preparation.

judge_stats.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: In `src/data_loader.py`, there is no actual standardization logic applied to data. The file only contains placeholder functions like `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and `build_transforms()` returning `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. These are just strings/placeholders, with no use of a scaler (e.g., `StandardScaler`) or any feature transformation code to ensure values are in the same range.

judge_stats.criteria: The elbow method is used to determine the optimal number of clusters. Please save the elbow plot to `results/figures/elbow.jpg`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although `results/figures/elbow.jpg` exists in the workspace tree, the provided evidence shows it is not a valid readable image: `Error reading image file ... "The image data you provided does not represent a valid image."` There is also no code snippet or trajectory evidence confirming that the elbow method was actually run to determine the optimal cluster count or that a proper elbow plot was successfully saved.

judge_stats.criteria: The K-means clustering algorithm is implemented in `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement K-means. The file only defines `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS = []`, `requirement_notes()`, and a placeholder `build_model()` that returns `"model placeholder"`. There is no clustering logic, no `KMeans` import or class, no fit/predict code, and no algorithm steps related to K-means in the provided code.

<UNSATISFIED>: Although `results/metrics/cluster_centers.txt` exists, its content is only `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is placeholder text rather than saved cluster center values. There is also no execution or save evidence showing actual KMeans cluster centers were written to this file.

judge_stats.criteria: The Customer segmentation is visualized using "seaborn," with the plot saved as `results/figures/customer_segmentation.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement is not supported by the provided evidence. Although a file named `results/figures/customer_segmentation.png` exists in the workspace tree, its content could not be read as a valid image (`"The image data you provided does not represent a valid image"`). Also, no code snippets from `src/model.py` or `src/data_loader.py` are provided showing use of `seaborn`, and the trajectory explicitly says there is no recorded evidence confirming successful generation of the visualization.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive dashboard which allows dynamic exploration of the segments is created using "Dash".

reason: <UNSATISFIED> There is no evidence of a Dash-based interactive dashboard. The workspace contains only `src/data_loader.py`, `src/model.py`, and static output files under `results/` (`customer_segmentation.png`, `elbow.jpg`, `cluster_centers.txt`). Neither source file imports or references `dash`, app layout, callbacks, or server startup code. The trajectory evidence also explicitly states no relevant Dash execution steps were found.
